claims.quote: Interested in visiting the United States for medical training or research?
claims.claimText: Landing-page prompt directed at international students interested in visiting the US for medical training or research at UAB.
claims.limitations: Landing page only; no eligibility, application pathway, cost, or duration stated on this source.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Page is the International Medical Education landing page targeting international students seeking US medical training/research — Tier 1 USCE-adjacent.

claims.quote: the Office of International Medical Education has developed many international partnerships and created numerous interdisciplinary educational programs to promote the global exchange of knowledge in medical education, training, and research.
claims.claimText: UAB Office of International Medical Education runs interdisciplinary educational programs in medical education, training, and research.
claims.limitations: Generic descriptive language; no specific observership/elective offer, eligibility, or application route on this page.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Office of International Medical Education describes training/research programs accessible to international medical learners — Tier 1 wedge.
claims.visibilityRationale: P102-0C deterministic detection; needs model A1/A2 reader (P102-0D) for PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE promotion

claims.quote: Our programs serve that purpose well, as trainees will eventually return to their home countries and will use their UAB experience to professionally advance and become academic and medical leaders.
claims.claimText: UAB frames its international programs around trainees who will return to their home countries after the UAB experience.
claims.limitations: This is mission/framing language, not an explicit eligibility requirement; may imply non-US residency intent.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Framing of international trainees returning home aligns with Tier 1 visiting student/observer USCE audience.
claims.visibilityRationale: P102-0C deterministic detection; needs model A1/A2 reader (P102-0D) for PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE promotion

claims.quote: International Medical EducationHeersink School of Medicine
claims.claimText: Source is on the Heersink School of Medicine domain, not a UAB Hospital institution-specific page; campus applicability to UAB Hospital is not stated.
claims.limitations: Medical school scope cannot be promoted to PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE for UAB Hospital without explicit campus-applicability proof.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Scope-conflict record, not content; medical-school-level page applied to a hospital institution.
claims.visibilityRationale: shadow/volunteer is not auto-USCE; human review required

claims.quote: International Visiting Medical Students (IVMS) was created with a goal to strengthen and further the mission of the UAB Heersink School of Medicine by connecting international students with our globally recognized and nationally ranked faculty.
claims.claimText: UAB Heersink School of Medicine runs IVMS program for international medical students.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Page describes a visiting medical student elective program for current international medical students — pre-residency Tier 1.
claims.limitations: Page is hosted on UAB Heersink School of Medicine; campus applicability to UAB Hospital not explicitly stated on this page.
claims.visibilityRationale: A3 downgrade: IVMS source is uab.edu/medicine/international (Heersink School of Medicine) attributed to UAB Hospital. Strong implicit campus link (IVMS clinical rotations occur at UAB Hospital) — retain PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE but record scope note. No downgrade applied.

claims.quote: You must be in the final year of training at your home school or you must have completed one year of core clerkship rotations.
claims.claimText: Applicants must be in final year or have completed one year of core clerkships.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility requirement for visiting medical student rotation.

claims.quote: You must be actively enrolled in a program leading to the equivalent of an MD or DO. (Graduates are not eligible for this program.)
claims.claimText: Must be actively enrolled in MD/DO-equivalent program; graduates are not eligible.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility carveout — graduates (IMGs without active enrollment) are explicitly excluded from this Tier 1 program.

claims.quote: You must have the approval of the administration of your home school to attend courses at UAB.
claims.claimText: Home-school administration approval required.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application requirement for visiting student program.

claims.quote: The maximum length of participation is eight (8) weeks.
claims.claimText: Maximum length of participation is 8 weeks.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Duration limit for visiting medical student elective rotations.

claims.quote: iBT (internet-based) score of 100 with a minimum of 22 on the speaking sectionPaper-based test score of 600
claims.claimText: TOEFL iBT 100 (22 speaking) or paper-based 600 required.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: English proficiency requirement for IVMS applicants.

claims.quote: TOEFL is waived only if English is your first language, or if your medical school teaches all classes and rotations in English.
claims.claimText: TOEFL waiver available if English is first language or medical school is taught in English.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility waiver detail.

claims.quote: The application fee of $350 (U.S.) is non-refundable.
claims.claimText: Application fee is $350, non-refundable.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application fee for visiting student program.

claims.quote: Cost per 4-week non-credit elective is $5,200*. This includes tuition, housing and transport to and from the airport (excludes travel to and from the U.S.).
claims.claimText: Cost per 4-week non-credit elective is $5,200 (includes tuition, housing, airport transport).
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Cost statement for IVMS elective rotation.

claims.quote: No credit is received from UAB Heersink School of Medicine for clinical electives.
claims.claimText: No academic credit awarded by UAB Heersink for these clinical electives.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Important program detail — non-credit elective.

claims.quote: recommending that students participating in the IVMS Program enter the United States on a B-1/B-2 Visa with passport stamp for B-1 (Business)
claims.claimText: IVMS participants advised to enter US on B-1/B-2 visa with B-1 passport stamp.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Visa guidance for visiting medical students.

claims.quote: Every international visiting medical student is automatically covered for mal-practice insurance of $3 million per aggregate year and $1 million per instance. This coverage is provided through UAB Professional Liability Trust Fund at no charge while you are enrolled in our program.
claims.claimText: Malpractice insurance ($3M aggregate/$1M per instance) provided automatically at no charge.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Malpractice coverage details for visiting medical students.

claims.quote: Housing is included in the course fee and provided by UAB International Medical Education.
claims.claimText: Housing is included in the elective course fee.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Cost/benefit detail for visiting student program.

claims.quote: All cancellations must be received in writing by email to ivms@uab.edu
claims.claimText: Program contact email: ivms@uab.edu.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Coordinator contact email for the visiting student program.

claims.quote: Curriculum vitae/resumeCopy of your current official transcriptStatement of Approval from Dean of StudentsPhoto (only jpgs less than 250KB)Copy of the biographical information page of your passportPersonal StatementHealth Compliance Documentation
claims.claimText: Required application documents: CV, transcript, Dean of Students approval, photo, passport, personal statement, health compliance.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application document checklist for the IVMS program.

claims.quote: Only the most competitive applications will be scheduled for a virtual online interview.
claims.claimText: Competitive applicants are scheduled for a virtual online interview.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Selection process detail.

claims.quote: Upon completion of this program, visiting students will receive:Letter of completion
claims.claimText: Letter of completion issued upon program completion.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Output deliverable from visiting student program.

claims.quote: Please note: Fees cannot be adjusted, and we do not offer scholarships.
claims.claimText: Fees cannot be adjusted; no scholarships offered.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Financial policy detail.

claims.quote: (Graduates are not eligible for this program.)
claims.claimText: Graduates of medical school are explicitly not eligible for the IVMS program.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit refusal of graduates (post-MD IMGs) for visiting student program; observership pathway separate.
claims.limitations: Source scope is medical-school-level; refusal applies only to this IVMS program at UAB Heersink, not necessarily to UAB Hospital observerships.

claims.quote: International Visiting Medical Students (IVMS) was created with a goal to strengthen and further the mission of the UAB Heersink School of Medicine by connecting international students with our globally recognized and nationally ranked faculty.
claims.claimText: Source is UAB Heersink School of Medicine (medical school scope); institution is UAB Hospital — campus applicability not explicitly stated.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Page is a medical-school-level program (UAB Heersink). Rotations likely happen at UAB Hospital but the page does not explicitly name UAB Hospital as the clinical site.
claims.visibilityRationale: A3 downgrade: claimType is SCOPE_CONFLICT documenting medical-school vs hospital scope mismatch; cannot be PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE.

claims.quote: Are you a current medical student at UAB looking to travel abroad to expand your international medical knowledge?
claims.claimText: Office of International Medical Education also serves outbound UAB students traveling abroad — page scope is bidirectional, not USCE-inbound only.
claims.limitations: Outbound-student content is not USCE; relevant only as scope context.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Documents the dual scope of the office on the same landing page A1 read for inbound-international claims.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 focused on the inbound 'International Students' tile and skipped the parallel outbound 'UAB Students' tile, which materially changes the office scope interpretation.
claims.visibilityRationale: A3 downgrade: Quote 'Are you a current medical student at UAB looking to travel abroad...' is about OUTBOUND UAB students, not inbound USCE visitors. Not a USCE offer at all; classified MISSING_FIELD lane bidirectional.

claims.quote: Our mission is to create and foster global partnerships to develop innovative, collaborative and interdisciplinary educational programs and establish UAB as a leader in the training of global healthcare providers.
claims.claimText: Office mission statement asserts UAB position as leader in training global healthcare providers via partnership-based educational programs.
claims.limitations: Mission language; no specific elective/observership offering, eligibility, or pathway stated.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Tier 1 USCE-adjacent mission framing for an international-medical-education office.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured the 'About' descriptive paragraph but not the distinct mission-statement sentence under the 'Training global healthcare providers' tagline.
claims.visibilityRationale: P102-0C deterministic detection; needs model A1/A2 reader (P102-0D) for PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE promotion

claims.quote: to promote the global exchange of knowledge to create and sustain relationships with physicians and scientists across the globe
claims.claimText: UAB frames its international audience as physicians and scientists globally — broader audience signal than medical students alone.
claims.limitations: Mission framing; not an explicit eligibility statement. Implies audience includes post-graduate physicians and researchers.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Audience framing relevant to pre-residency USCE classification; disambiguates 'scholar' programs as potentially physician/researcher targeted.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured the 'return home' clause (c06) but missed the parallel audience phrase identifying the global audience as physicians and scientists.
claims.visibilityRationale: P102-0C deterministic detection; needs model A1/A2 reader (P102-0D) for PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE promotion

claims.quote: UAB requires all international visiting medical students to have medical health insurance. You may purchase UAB Student Health Insurance or provide your own. Non-UAB health insurance must be approved by UAB Student Health before arrival.
claims.claimText: Medical health insurance required; can purchase UAB Student Health Insurance or provide own (approved by UAB Student Health).
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Health insurance is a pre-arrival eligibility requirement for the IVMS visiting student program.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured malpractice but missed the separate Health Insurance Coverage subsection.

claims.quote: You must provide a copy of your current transcript written in English or accompanied by an English translation.
claims.claimText: Current transcript must be in English or accompanied by English translation.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility/document requirement for the visiting student application.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 listed documents generally but missed the explicit English-language transcript requirement.

claims.quote: We will only accept TOEFL scores that have been taken within a two (2) year period from the date that the application was submitted.
claims.claimText: TOEFL scores must have been taken within 2 years of application submission.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: TOEFL score validity rule — eligibility detail beyond the score thresholds A1 captured.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured TOEFL thresholds and waivers but missed the 2-year validity window.

claims.quote: Payment can be made by a major credit card or wire transfer.
claims.claimText: Payment accepted by major credit card or wire transfer.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Cost logistics — payment method detail for IVMS fees.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured fee amounts but omitted the payment-method sentence.

claims.quote: For cancellation of your elective more than 90 calendar days before the start date of the elective, only half of your course elective fee will be refunded, minus any administrative or financial processing fees.
claims.claimText: Cancellation >90 days before start: 50% refund minus fees; <90 days: no refund.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Refund/cancellation policy for visiting student program.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 noted no scholarships but didn't capture the cancellation/refund tiers.

claims.quote: The IVMS program has a strict policy for arriving and departing. Arrival is the Sunday before electives and departure is the Saturday at the end of the elective, before 11am.
claims.claimText: Strict arrival/departure: arrive Sunday before elective; depart Saturday at end before 11am.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Logistics policy specific to the IVMS visiting student program.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 didn't capture the arrival/departure logistics paragraph.

claims.quote: Student Health Form Acknowledging that you must receive medical clearance prior to training. More details/instructions about how to upload documentation will be sent in pre-matriculation email sent 8 weeks prior to start date.
claims.claimText: Medical clearance required prior to training; instructions sent in pre-matriculation email 8 weeks prior.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Health clearance / immunization-style requirement for IVMS participation.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1's document list mentioned 'Health Compliance Documentation' but didn't extract the explicit medical-clearance requirement and 8-week pre-matric window.

claims.quote: Elective preferences for 1 or 2 rotations based on availability in catalogue
claims.claimText: Applicants submit preferences for 1 or 2 rotations based on catalogue availability.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application pathway step — rotation preference selection.
claims.whyA1Missed: A1 grouped documents but didn't note the 1-2 rotation preference selection step.
